title: dactylorchis orientalis subsp. foliosa
intro:
Dactylorchis orientalis subsp. foliosa, also known as the leafy Dactylorhiza, is a subspecies of orchid native to various regions, particularly in Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and yellow-green flowers.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. It has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and boosted immune system.

Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area. However, it is essential to note that the use of this plant should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist, due to potential interactions with other medications and individual sensitivities.
